description: Define the new-install bootstrap boundary: host bootstrap only installs/starts k3s, imports baked control-plane assets, applies local storage/control-plane manifests, exposes setup, and provides fallback commands.
Create a Kubernetes appliance control-plane manifest/chart that runs setup/status/update components in a dedicated namespace independent of the Alga PSA application namespace.
Package the current setup/status UI for Kubernetes-hosted delivery while preserving the existing user-facing setup and status experience.
Package the setup/status API logic for Kubernetes-hosted execution, reusing current endpoint contracts where practical.
Provide Kubernetes RBAC/service account configuration that allows the control plane to perform setup operations with the narrowest practical permissions for v1.
Stage baked control-plane manifests and image archive(s) into the Ubuntu ISO build output so first setup works without registry pulls.
Update host bootstrap scripts to install/start k3s with minimal options and wait for the Kubernetes API before applying control-plane assets.
Update host bootstrap scripts to import baked control-plane image archive(s) into k3s/containerd idempotently.
Update host bootstrap scripts to apply local storage and control-plane manifests from the ISO-staged filesystem path, not repository-relative paths.
Expose the Kubernetes-hosted setup UI on the existing appliance setup port, currently 8080, without requiring external network access.
Preserve setup token generation and token enforcement for setup/status access in the Kubernetes-hosted control plane.
Move or bridge setup state persistence so the Kubernetes-hosted control plane can resume setup and report progress across pod restarts.
Ensure the control plane can validate setup inputs, persist release/runtime selection, configure Flux source, configure runtime values, and trigger application bootstrap.
Ensure the control plane creates and protects the initial tenant/admin setup Secret used by the bootstrap job.
Ensure status reporting remains available when the Alga PSA application namespace, HelmRelease, pods, or bootstrap job are unhealthy.
Add a tiny host fallback command or script that reapplies the baked control-plane bundle from local disk and reports basic host/k3s diagnostics.
Update console output to describe substrate bootstrap, setup UI availability, and fallback recovery commands.
Update support bundle collection to include host bootstrap logs, control-plane pod logs, control-plane resources, and application bootstrap resources.
Keep existing host-based setup behavior out of the new-install path except for fallback/recovery pieces, avoiding duplicate primary setup services.
Document the new bootstrap architecture and new-install operational runbook for appliance support and development.
